detection_signals: A vigorous canopy always makes bad wine
Low vigor means higher quality fruit automatically
You can't make quality wine from vigorous vines
why_incorrect: Canopy vigor and fruit quality are related but not in a simple proportion. Moderate vigor with good balance produces quality grapes. Canopy management practices can achieve optimal structure regardless of vigor.
misconception: Higher canopy vigor (more leaf growth) always produces lower quality fruit because the vine prioritizes vegetative growth over fruit ripening.
corrected_understanding: Moderate canopy vigor with good balance and management practices produces quality fruit. Canopy quality depends on management, not vigor level alone.
